scenario: Soft lockup/hard lockup during out-of-tree kernel module loading/unloading
symptoms: watchdog: BUG: soft lockup - CPU#1 stuck for 134s! [insmod:1141]
Kernel panic - not syncing: Hard LOCKUP

workflow.thought: Check kernel version to confirm affected release

workflow.observation: Size should be 0x320 in affected kernel
pv_lock_ops offset at 0x2f0 (should be 0x2e8 in older kernels)
pv_lock_ops at offset 0x2f0 (mismatched with older modules)

root_cause_analysis: An out-of-tree (O) kernel module built using the kernel-devel version lower than 4.18.0-425.3.1.el8 may fail to load/unload with soft lockup/hard lockup panic or hang the system running RHEL-8.7 kernel version 4.18.0-425.3.1.el8 due to change of the function pointer offsets in the pv_lock_ops structure within the paravirt_patch_template structure.

In the RHEL-8.7 kernel version 4.18.0-425.3.1.el8, the insertion of a new method pointer notify_page_enc_status_changed into pv_mmu_ops structure increased its size by 8 bytes. This change pushed all the method pointers in pv_mmu_ops and pv_lock_ops off by 8, leading to soft lockup/hard lockup when an older kernel module is performing a locking operation.
